text: Code reviews are a vital part of software development. They involve team members checking each other's code for mistakes. This practice helps catch bugs early, long before the software reaches users. Fixing a bug at this stage is much faster and cheaper than fixing it later. Besides finding errors, code reviews allow team members to share knowledge and learn new techniques. A developer might suggest a better way to solve a problem, helping everyone improve. Ultimately, this process builds a culture of collaboration and raises the overall quality of the team's work.
